output.summary: Add a 'related' option to the postsSource attribute. In 'related' mode, the block queries posts that share taxonomy terms with the current post being viewed. No editor controls for taxonomy/post selection are shown — the relation is automatic based on the current post's terms. The PHP template must detect the current post context and build a taxonomy query dynamically.

fileChanges.description: Add 'related' to the postsSource enum and add new attributes for related mode configuration
fileChanges.details: 1. Update postsSource enum from ['automatic', 'manual'] to ['automatic', 'manual', 'related']. 2. Add 'relatedPostsLimit' attribute (number, default 9) — or reuse existing postsLimit. 3. Add 'relatedFallbackToLatest' attribute (boolean, default true) — controls whether to fall back to latest posts of the same type if no related posts are found. 4. Add 'excludeCurrent' attribute (boolean, default true) — always exclude the current post from results.

fileChanges.description: Add 'Related' option to the Selection Mode toggle group and conditionally show related-mode controls
fileChanges.details: 1. Add a third ToggleGroupControlOption with value='related' and label='Related' to the Selection Mode toggle group. 2. When postsSource === 'related', show: (a) a notice/help text explaining that related posts are determined automatically based on the current post's taxonomy terms, (b) the postsLimit RangeControl (reuse existing), (c) the hideIfEmpty toggle, (d) a 'Fallback to latest' toggle (relatedFallbackToLatest). 3. Hide taxonomy multi-selects and PostSelectorSortable when in related mode. 4. Update the TypeScript type to include new attributes. 5. The editor preview via FilteredServerSideRender will work because PHP handles the query — no special editor-side logic needed beyond passing attributes.

fileChanges.description: Add related posts query logic that finds posts sharing taxonomy terms with the current post
fileChanges.details: Add a new branch for postsSource === 'related': 1. Get the current post ID via get_the_ID() (works on frontend; in editor SSR context, use the global $post or the block's context). 2. Determine the active post type from postsType attribute. 3. Look up the current post's terms for the primary taxonomy associated with the postsType (using the taxonomy_filters config — e.g., 'category' for posts, 'department' for team_member, etc.). 4. Build a tax_query using those term IDs. 5. Exclude the current post via 'post__not_in'. 6. Use postsLimit for posts_per_page. 7. If no related posts found and relatedFallbackToLatest is true, fall back to a simple latest-posts query (no taxonomy filter) for that post type, still excluding current post. 8. Handle edge case: if not on a singular context (e.g., archive page, homepage), treat 'related' like 'automatic' or show nothing based on hideIfEmpty.

attributeChanges.name: postsSource
attributeChanges.description: Add 'related' to the enum: ['automatic', 'manual', 'related']. Related mode queries posts that share taxonomy terms with the currently viewed post.

attributeChanges.name: relatedFallbackToLatest
attributeChanges.description: When in 'related' mode and no related posts are found, fall back to showing the latest posts of the same post type. Prevents empty carousels on posts with no shared taxonomy terms.

attributeChanges.name: excludeCurrent
attributeChanges.description: In 'related' mode, exclude the currently viewed post from carousel results. Default true — almost always desired to avoid showing the post the user is already reading.

designDecisions.question: How should 'related' mode determine relatedness?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use the primary taxonomy for the selected postsType (category for posts, department for team_member, audience for resource, policy_topic for policy). Query posts that share ANY term with the current post in that taxonomy. This leverages the existing taxonomy_filters config in PHP.
designDecisions.decision: Related posts are determined by shared taxonomy terms using the primary taxonomy for the selected post type.
designDecisions.evidence: The PHP template already has a per-post-type taxonomy_filters config that maps each post type to its primary taxonomy. Reusing this mapping is consistent and avoids adding new configuration.

designDecisions.question: What should happen when there are no related posts (no shared terms)?
designDecisions.recommendation: Add a 'relatedFallbackToLatest' boolean attribute (default true). When enabled, fall back to the latest posts of the same type. This prevents empty carousels. When disabled, respect hideIfEmpty behavior.
designDecisions.decision: Fall back to latest posts of the same type when no related posts exist, controlled by a toggle.
designDecisions.evidence: The user requested a 'related' mode. Empty carousels are a poor UX, so a fallback mechanism with a toggle gives editors control.

designDecisions.question: What should happen when the block is placed on a non-singular page (homepage, archive)?
designDecisions.recommendation: On non-singular pages, there is no 'current post' to relate to. The block should either: (a) show nothing and respect hideIfEmpty, or (b) fall back to automatic mode behavior. Recommend option (a) — show a helpful message in the editor and render nothing on the frontend unless fallback is enabled.
designDecisions.evidence: This is an edge case where the related mode has no context. The user should decide the desired behavior.

designDecisions.question: Should 'related' mode reuse the existing postsLimit attribute or have its own limit?
designDecisions.recommendation: Reuse the existing postsLimit attribute. There is no design reason for a separate limit. This simplifies the attribute surface and the postsLimit control can be shown for both 'automatic' and 'related' modes.
designDecisions.evidence: The postsLimit attribute already exists and serves the same purpose. Adding a separate relatedPostsLimit would be redundant.

designDecisions.question: Should related mode use ALL taxonomies for a post type or just the primary one?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use only the primary taxonomy per post type (matching the existing taxonomy_filters config). For 'resource' which has two taxonomies (audience + resource_type), use both — a post is 'related' if it shares terms in ANY of the post type's taxonomies. This uses an OR relation in the tax_query.
designDecisions.decision: Use all configured taxonomies for the post type with OR relation.
designDecisions.evidence: The taxonomy_filters config already defines which taxonomies are relevant per post type. Using all of them provides better relatedness matching.

editorBehavior.relatedModeControls: When postsSource === 'related', show: (1) an informational Notice explaining the mode, (2) postsLimit RangeControl, (3) hideIfEmpty toggle, (4) relatedFallbackToLatest toggle, (5) excludeCurrent toggle. Hide: PostSelectorSortable, TaxonomyMultiSelect components.
editorBehavior.editorPreview: FilteredServerSideRender will call the PHP template which runs the related query. In the editor SSR context, the global $post should be available if the user is editing a post. If editing a page or in the site editor, the preview may show fallback content or be empty.

output.qaNotes: Test on: (1) a single post page with categories assigned — verify related posts appear, (2) a post with no categories — verify fallback behavior, (3) homepage/archive context — verify graceful handling, (4) each post type in related mode, (5) editor preview renders correctly, (6) switching between automatic/manual/related modes preserves other settings correctly.
